name: Group Theory ‚Äî Axioms & Basic Theorems
description: First layers of group theory: four axioms (closure, associativity, identity, inverse), then basic theorems with explicit dependencies. Layer 1: uniqueness of identity and inverse, cancellation laws. Layer 2: inverse of inverse, inverse of product, two-sided identity. Layer 3: uniqueness of solutions to ax=b and ya=b, general associativity. Layer 4: power laws a‚Åøa·µê=a‚Åø‚Å∫·µê and (a‚Åø)·µê=a‚Åø·µê. Mirrors Euclid's Elements structure‚Äîaxioms at base, theorems build in layers.
mermaid:
graph TD
    G1["G1 Closure\nab in G for all a b in G"]
    G2["G2 Associativity\na bc = ab c"]
    G3["G3 Identity\nexists e ea = ae = a"]
    G4["G4 Inverse\nfor each a exists a inv aa inv = e"]
    T1["T1 Uniqueness\nof identity"]
    T2["T2 Uniqueness\nof inverse"]
    T3["T3 Left cancellation\nab = ac implies b = c"]
    T4["T4 Right cancellation\nba = ca implies b = c"]
    T5["T5 a inv inv = a"]
    T6["T6 ab inv = b inv a inv"]
    T7["T7 Identity two-sided\nea = a iff ae = a"]
    T8["T8 ax = b has unique x"]
    T9["T9 ya = b has unique y"]
    T10["T10 General associativity\na1...an well-defined"]
    T11["T11 Power product\na^n a^m = a^(n+m)"]
    T12["T12 Power power\n(a^n)^m = a^(nm)"]
    T13["T13 Negative power\na^(-n) = (a^-1)^n"]
